menace 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

menace

menace provides text-formatting tools to enhance your chat game and unleash your inner menace (and at times, help save your coworkers from corporate depression).

Installation

You can install this package using

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("adamoshen/menace")

or

# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("adamoshen/menace")

Introduction 

library(menace)

menace works by formatting text depending on the action called. It is then up to the user to perform any stylistic formattings and then copy the result to the clipboard, e.g. with clipr’s write_clip() function.

Designed with Microsoft Teams in mind as the output destination, this package includes Markdown formatters compatible with Microsoft Teams. For other destinations, consider the usage of the Markdown functions contained in gluedown.

The intended use case of menace is for single strings. However, the functions are compatible with character vectors.

Example usage - mock() 

"This package is amazing!" |>
  mock() |>
  teams_italic()
#> [1] "_tHiS PaCkAgE Is aMaZiNg!_"

Example usage - shout() 

"This package is amazing!" |>
  shout() |>
  teams_bold()
#> [1] "*T H I S    P A C K A G E    I S    A M A Z I N G !*"

Example usage - shout() + mock() 

"This package is amazing!" |>
  shout() |>
  mock(method = "random") |>
  teams_bold()
#> [1] "*t H i S    P a c k a G e    I S    a m A Z i n g !*"
